data.title: Air Transport Services reports mixed Q2 results; raises FY24 Adjusted EBITDA outlook
data.description:
• Air Transport Services press release (NASDAQ:ATSG): Q2 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.19 beats by $0.03.
  • Revenue of $488M (-7.8% Y/Y) misses by $25.67M.
  • Adjusted EBITDA of $130.4 million, versus $157.1 million.
  • Free Cash Flow was $91.8 million, versus negative $1.3 million.

  • 2024 Outlook:

    Taking into account the four aircraft leases that commenced since the end of June, ATSG expects Adjusted EBITDA of approximately $526 million in 2024.

  • ATSG expects capital spending for 2024 to be $390 million, down from the $410 million estimated in May 2024, and down $400 million from 2023 actual spending. ATSG's total projected capital spend includes growth capital of $225 million. ATSG expects third quarter Adjusted EBITDA to be similar to the second quarter as ramp-up costs for adding ten Amazon supplied 767 freighters continue, but higher in the fourth quarter when the last few enter service and other peak-season operations occur.

  • "We are ahead of our target for positive free cash flow for the year, with $107 million generated in the first half and an expectation to add to that total in the second half."

data.title: Arcus Biosciences GAAP EPS of -$1.02 beats by $0.11, revenue of $39M beats by $12.51M
data.description:
• Arcus Biosciences press release (NYSE:RCUS): Q2 GAAP EPS of -$1.02 beats by $0.11.
  • Revenue of $39M (+34.5% Y/Y) beats by $12.51M.
  • Cash, Cash Equivalents and Marketable Securities were $1.0 billion as of June 30, 2024, compared to $866 million as of December 31, 2023.
  • "We believe our cash, cash equivalents and marketable securities on-hand will be sufficient to fund operations into 2027."
  • Cash, cash equivalents and marketable securities are expected to be between $885 million and $925 million at the end of 2024.

data.title: Ingles Markets Non-GAAP EPS of $1.67, revenue of $1.39B
data.description:
• Ingles Markets press release (NASDAQ:IMKTA): Q3 Non-GAAP EPS of $1.67.
  • Revenue of $1.39B (-2.8% Y/Y).
  • The company currently has no outstanding borrowings under its $150.0 million line of credit.
  • The company believes its financial resources, including its line of credit and other internal and external sources of funds, will be sufficient to meet planned capital expenditures, debt service and working capital requirements for the foreseeable future.

data.title: GoGold Resources GAAP EPS of $0.002, revenue of $10.4M
data.description:
• GoGold Resources press release (OTCQX:GLGDF): Q3 GAAP EPS of $0.002.
  • Revenue of $10.4M (+22.5% Y/Y) from the sale of 365,119 silver equivalent ounces.
  • Production of 400,236 silver equivalent ounces, consisting of 138,708 silver ounces, 2,436 gold ounces, 148 copper tonnes, and 125 zinc tonnes.
  • Adjusted cash cost per silver equivalent ounce of $18.54.
  • Adjusted all in sustaining cost per silver equivalent ounce of $24.59.

data.title: PureCycle Technologies reports Q2 results
data.description:
• PureCycle Technologies press release (NASDAQ:PCT): ended the second quarter of fiscal 2024 with approximately $10.9 million of unrestricted cash.
  • On August 7, PureCycle reached an agreement on the sale of $22.5 million notional amount of its Southern Ohio Port Authority Revenue Bonds that will provide cash proceeds of $18.0 million.

data.title: IAMGOLD beats top-line and bottom-line estimates; updates FY24 outlook
data.description:
• IAMGOLD press release (NYSE:IAG): Q2 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.16 beats by $0.07.
  • Revenue of $385.3M (+61.3% Y/Y) beats by $37.15M from sales of 167,000 ounces at an average realized gold price of $2,294 per ounce.
  • Attributable gold production was 166,000 ounces in the second quarter.
  • Net cash from operating activities was $160.1 million for the second quarter.
  • Adjusted EBITDA was $191.1 million during the second quarter.

  • Outlook: Production guidance at Essakane and Westwood has been increased to 495,000 to 540,000 ounces, up from the previous guidance range of 430,000 to 490,000 ounces.

  • Côté Gold production this year is expected to be on the lower end of the guidance range of 130,000 to 175,000 ounces on a 60.3% basis (220,000 to 290,000 ounces at 100%) as improvements to mill availability are made during the ramp-up of operations.

  • The cash cost guidance range, excluding Côté Gold, is revised downwards from $1,280 to $1,400 per ounce sold to $1,175 to $1,275 per ounce sold and all-in-sustaining-cost guidance range is also revised downwards from $1,780 to $1,940 per ounce sold to $1,700 to $1,825 per ounce sold.

    • For Côté Gold, the company continues to expect that as Côté exits the year at 90% throughput, cash costs at that time will be in the range of approximately $700 to $800 per ounce sold and AISC of $1,100 to $1,200 per ounce.

  • Côté Gold's capital expenditures in 2024 are expected to be higher than the life-of-mine average as the mine progresses the completion of the construction of the full tailings dam footprint to support the life of mine.
